2603.26359.title: Automated near-term quantum algorithm discovery for molecular ground states
2603.26359.paper_summary: Designing quantum algorithms is a complex and counterintuitive task, making it an ideal candidate for AI-driven algorithm discovery. To this end, we employ the Hive, an AI platform for program synthesis, which utilises large language models to drive a highly distributed evolutionary process for discovering new algorithms. We focus on the ground state problem in quantum chemistry, and discover efficient quantum heuristic algorithms that solve it for molecules LiH, H2O, and F2 while exhibiting significant reductions in quantum resources relative to state-of-the-art near-term quantum algorithms. Further, we perform an interpretability study on the discovered algorithms and identify the key functions responsible for the efficiency gains. Finally, we benchmark the Hive-discovered circuits on the Quantinuum System Model H2 quantum computer and identify minimum system requirements for chemical precision. We envision that this novel approach to quantum algorithm discovery applies to other domains beyond chemistry, as well as to designing quantum algorithms for fault-tolerant quantum computers.
2603.26359.paper_summary_zh: 設計量子算法是一項複雜且違反直覺的任務，使其成為AI驅動算法發現的理想候選者。為此，我們使用Hive，一個用於程式合成的AI平台，它利用大型語言模型推動一個高度分散的進化過程來發現新算法。我們專注於量子化學中的基態問題，並發現有效的量子啟發式算法，能夠解決LiH、H2O和F2分子的問題，同時相較於最先進的近端量子算法顯示出顯著減少的量子資源。此外，我們對發現的算法進行了可解釋性研究，並確定了負責效率提升的關鍵功能。最後，我們在Quantinuum System Model H2量子計算機上對Hive發現的電路進行基準測試，並確定了化學精度的最低系統要求。我們預見這種新穎的量子算法發現方法適用於化學以外的其他領域，以及為容錯量子計算機設計量子算法。
2603.26359.author: Fabian Finger et.al.
2603.26359.authors: Fabian Finger, Frederic Rapp, Pranav Kalidindi, Kerry He, Kante Yin, Alexander Koziell-Pipe, David Zsolt Manrique, Gabriel Greene-Diniz, Stephen Clark, Hamza Fawzi, Bernardino Romera Paredes, Alhussein Fawzi, Konstantinos Meichanetzidis
